name: fd-rl-training-pipeline
focus: RL training pipeline patterns in Atropos environments applicable to Autarch agent training
persona: An ML engineer specializing in reinforcement learning from environment feedback, with deep experience in Atropos/GRPO training loops and reward function design for agentic systems.
decision_lens: Prioritizes findings that reveal reusable abstractions (base env, reward API, toolset distribution) that Autarch could adopt without rebuilding infrastructure. Flags anything that couples training to Hermes-specific tooling.
review_areas: HermesAgentBaseEnv two-phase design (OpenAI server for Phase 1, ManagedServer/VLLM for Phase 2) and whether this maps to Autarch's training roadmap
ToolContext reward function pattern — same Modal sandbox used during rollout reused for reward verification
Toolset distribution sampling as implicit curriculum mechanism — stochastic toolset selection per group for task diversity
HermesAgentEnvConfig field structure: which config fields are environment-agnostic and should be promoted to Autarch's base config
Thread pool executor pattern for isolating sync tool calls using asyncio.run() internally — deadlock prevention in nested event loops
AgentResult metadata fields (turns_used, finished_naturally, tool_errors, reasoning_per_turn) as trajectory metadata schema
success_hints: Identify at least one config/API boundary in HermesAgentBaseEnv that Autarch could subclass directly
Find the reward function contract and assess if general enough for Autarch's task types
Flag Hermes-specific tool names hard-coded into agent loop that would need parameterization
task_context: Analyzing Hermes Agent to find patterns adaptable for Sylveste's Autarch (L3 agent orchestration) and intercom (messaging/gateway).
anti_overlap: fd-gateway-messaging covers gateway delivery, platform adapters, and channel directory
fd-mini-swe-agent covers execution environment abstraction and trajectory format bridge
fd-honcho-user-modeling covers cross-session memory and peer modeling

name: fd-gateway-messaging
focus: Gateway and messaging architecture patterns in Hermes for intercom's multi-platform delivery design
persona: A systems engineer with expertise in multi-platform messaging infrastructure, webhook routing, and async delivery pipelines.
decision_lens: Prioritizes architectural boundaries — platform-specific vs. platform-agnostic — and delivery reliability. Flags designs intercom could adopt wholesale vs. patterns needing rethinking for Rust/tokio-postgres.
review_areas: DeliveryTarget/DeliveryRouter design — origin/local/platform:chat_id address scheme and three-tier delivery model
Channel directory as lazy-built capability registry: startup enumeration for Discord/Slack vs. session-history inference for Telegram/WhatsApp
Platform adapter base class — minimal contract and portability to intercom's plugin model
HookRegistry event system — HOOK.yaml + handler.py discovery, wildcard matching, error isolation
Session mirroring and pairing system — code-based approval, rate limiting, lockout, secure permissions
Cron job delivery routing — how scheduled agent outputs route to messaging targets
success_hints: Determine if platform:chat_id address scheme is compatible with intercom's channel model
Assess if gateway hook system could serve as lightweight plugin protocol for intercom
Identify platform-specific assumptions that won't generalize to intercom's multi-tenant model
task_context: Analyzing Hermes Agent to find patterns adaptable for Sylveste's Autarch (L3 agent orchestration) and intercom (messaging/gateway).
anti_overlap: fd-rl-training-pipeline covers Atropos environments, reward functions, two-phase training
fd-security-patterns covers pairing system security properties in depth
fd-honcho-user-modeling covers session identity and user modeling

name: fd-security-patterns
focus: Security patterns in Hermes beyond what Sylveste has already captured — redaction, auth, sandboxing
persona: A security engineer focused on secrets management, credential isolation, and least-privilege design in agentic systems.
decision_lens: Prioritizes patterns that plug specific gaps in Sylveste's current security posture. De-prioritizes anything already covered by existing safety floors or interspect.
review_areas: redact.py layered regex redaction: prefix patterns (sk-, ghp_, xox*), ENV assignments, JSON fields, Authorization headers, Telegram tokens
PairingStore security: unambiguous alphabet, secrets.choice(), 1-hour TTL, 3 pending cap, rate limiting, lockout, 0o600 permissions
_secure_write() pattern (atomic write + chmod 0o600) — which state files use it and which don't
Terminal backend isolation hierarchy: local vs. docker vs. modal vs. ssh vs. singularity security guarantees
Tool name allowlist enforcement — how unknown tool names are rejected vs. ignored as prompt injection guardrail
.env loading priority chain — credential isolation guarantees in multi-tenant deployments
success_hints: Identify redaction patterns Sylveste's logging doesn't implement
Determine if PairingStore lockout mechanism is absent from Sylveste auth flows
Find inconsistent _secure_write() usage revealing security boundary gaps
task_context: Analyzing Hermes Agent to find patterns adaptable for Sylveste's Autarch (L3 agent orchestration) and intercom (messaging/gateway).
anti_overlap: fd-gateway-messaging covers gateway delivery and hook system architecture
fd-rl-training-pipeline covers agent loop from ML/training perspective
fd-honcho-user-modeling covers session identity, not credential management

name: fd-mini-swe-agent
focus: Mini-swe-agent execution environment abstraction and trajectory bridge as model for Autarch's sandboxed task execution
persona: An agent infrastructure engineer specializing in sandboxed execution environments for LLM-driven software tasks.
decision_lens: Prioritizes findings about execution backend interface contracts, trajectory format normalization, and batch pipeline design. Flags assumptions that would break with new backends.
review_areas: create_environment() factory — how local/docker/modal are unified behind execute() interface and minimal backend contract
MINI_SWE_AGENT_FINAL_OUTPUT sentinel signal — stdout scanning vs. tool_calls cessation for completion detection
_convert_to_hermes_format() trajectory bridge — OpenAI tool_calls to ShareGPT from/value with XML tags, losslessness
run_batch() design — immediate JSONL flush, error isolation per task, implications for Autarch's batch eval
patches.py async-safe workaround for SwerexModalEnvironment — thread executor for nested event loops
TerminalbenchEnv and HermesSweEnv as concrete subclasses — minimal interface for new SWE environments
success_hints: Determine if trajectory bridge output is compatible with batch_runner + trajectory_compressor pipeline
Identify if execute() backend interface is formally documented or duck-typed
Assess if sentinel approach is robust enough or if structured completion protocol is preferable
task_context: Analyzing Hermes Agent to find patterns adaptable for Sylveste's Autarch (L3 agent orchestration) and intercom (messaging/gateway).
anti_overlap: fd-rl-training-pipeline covers Atropos base env and reward functions
fd-security-patterns covers terminal isolation from security perspective
fd-gateway-messaging covers platform delivery, not execution environments

name: fd-honcho-user-modeling
focus: Honcho cross-session user modeling architecture as pattern for Autarch's agent identity and memory layer
persona: An AI systems architect specializing in long-term agent memory, user representation, and cross-session context continuity.
decision_lens: Prioritizes patterns addressing gaps in Sylveste's current session/memory model. De-prioritizes Honcho-specific API details in favor of architectural concepts.
review_areas: Peer/session/workspace primitive model — how user_peer_id and assistant_peer_id separate identity from session, asymmetric observe_me/observe_others
context() call for semantic prefetch — single API returning peer_representation, peer_card, message history with semantic search
new_session() vs. delete() behavior — preserving old session for continued modeling vs. clearing local cache
migrate_local_history() and migrate_memory_files() — XML-wrapped transcript upload for bootstrapping from local MEMORY.md
HonchoClientConfig resolution chain — host-block > flat global > defaults, auto-enable-when-key-present
Linked workspaces pattern — single config spanning multiple agent hosts sharing user modeling data
success_hints: Determine if Honcho's peer+session model fits Sylveste better than current session-keyed approach
Assess if migrate_local_history() XML approach bridges Sylveste's existing conversation history
Identify session_strategy assumptions about single-project vs. multi-project deployments
task_context: Analyzing Hermes Agent to find patterns adaptable for Sylveste's Autarch (L3 agent orchestration) and intercom (messaging/gateway).
anti_overlap: fd-rl-training-pipeline covers training loop and environment config
fd-gateway-messaging covers platform routing, not memory architecture
fd-security-patterns covers credential handling, not user modeling
